---
title: "Changing the Date Format Displayed by Quarto"
description: "A note on changing the displayed date format in Quarto with the `date-format` option."
date: "2026-01-30"
date-modified: "2026-01-30"
draft: false
lang: en
categories: [r, quarto]
---

::: {.callout-note}
Original Japanese version: [Quartoで表示される日付型を変更する](../../../posts/2026-01-30-quarto-data-format/index.qmd)
:::

When Quarto displays dates for blog posts, the default format is the medium date style.
This looks like **Jan 30, 2026**.
However, there are times when you may want to display dates in an [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) style such as **2026-01-30**.

## The `date-format` Option

To change how Quarto displays dates, set the `date-format` option.
You can change the displayed date format by specifying this option in a YAML header or in `_quarto.yml`.
The place where this is most commonly used is probably the listing page for blog posts.

When listing multiple articles, such as blog posts, add the `date-format` option to the YAML header of `posts.qmd`.

```{.yaml filename="posts.qmd"}
---
title: "Blog"
listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: default
  categories: true
  date-format: iso
---
```

If you want to change the date format on individual article pages, add the `date-format` option to each article's YAML header.

```{.yaml filename="posts/2026-01-30-quarto-data-format.qmd"}
---
title: "Changing the Date Format Displayed by Quarto"
date: "2026-01-30"
date-modified: "2026-01-30"
date-format: iso
---
```

You can also add it as a global setting in `_quarto.yml`.

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
date-format: iso
```

## Types of Date Formats

There are two ways to change the display format.

1. Specify a date style.
2. Specify a custom format.

### Specifying a Date Style

Quarto supports date styles such as the following.

- `short`: short format, for example `1/30/26`
- `medium`: medium format, for example `Jan 30, 2026`
- `long`: long format, for example `January 30, 2026`
- `full`: full format, for example `Friday, January 30, 2026`
- `iso`: ISO 8601 format, for example `2026-01-30`

### Specifying a Custom Format

If you want finer control over the style, use a custom format.
To specify a custom format, set a format string in the `date-format` option.
For example, to display dates as `2026/01/30`, specify the following.

```{.yaml}
date-format: "yyyy/MM/dd"
```

::: {.callout-note}
For details on custom formats, see the following official documentation.

- [Quarto Dates and Date Formatting](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/dates.html)
:::

### Differences by Language Setting

In Quarto, the displayed date format is also affected by the language setting.
For example, with a Japanese setting, the `medium` style is displayed like **2026年1月30日**.
To change the language setting, set the `lang` option in a YAML header or in `_quarto.yml`.

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
format:
  html:
    lang: ja
```

It may also be fine to set it at the top level.

::: {.callout-note}
For details on the `lang` option, see the following official documentation.

- [Document Language - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/language.html)
:::

## References

- [Document Listings - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-listings.html#listing-options)
- [Quarto Dates and Date Formatting](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/dates.html)
- [Document Language - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/language.html)
- [HTML Options - Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/formats/html.html#language)
